text: When building a web application, what is your primary focus?

options.text: Conducting user research, assessing ROI, and setting product requirements.
Architecting secure API routes, managing databases, and processing business logic.
Structuring simple navigation, creating clear instructions, and ensuring learning accessibility.
Setting up build automation, managing servers, and maintaining continuous delivery.

text: What type of problem are you most comfortable solving in your daily work?

options.text: Fixing broken ETL pipelines, cleaning messy CSV columns, and optimizing aggregate queries.
Fixing low course completion rates and designing simple materials for struggling learners.
Diagnosing digital health record mismatches and maintaining system uptime for medical staff.
Tuning hyper-parameters, resolving model overfitting, and handling biased training data.

text: Which software development tool or technology do you use or study the most?

Server frameworks and databases like Node.js, C#, PostgreSQL, or Redis.
Integrated systems like Next.js, full-stack frameworks, and monolithic web packages.

text: What would make you feel most proud at the completion of a major project?

Safety-checking API access scopes, token hashing, and route security keys.
Auditing database access permissions and tracking row modifications.
Auditing design layouts for accessibility contrast and keyboard navigation.

text: How do you design login systems for security?

options.text: Securing session storage tokens and designing clear error validation warnings.
Applying multi-signature biometric checks for medical diagnostic data access.
Implementing secure password hashing (BCrypt), JWT scopes, and api rate limits.
Assessing risk rules to balance security against customer registration conversion.

text: What is your approach to handling customer user delete requests (GDPR)?

options.text: Designing user-friendly deletion confirmation screens and account toggles.
Ensuring server storage backups are configured to process data deletes safely.
Archiving student grade records securely while purging personal profile details.
Consulting legal experts to verify privacy policy updates are correct.

text: How do you secure server-to-server communications?

options.text: Negotiating security agreements with corporate partners and providers.
Using secure gRPC routes, server API keys, and verifying token origins.
Encrypting model training data pipelines across cloud processing servers.
Securing data routes between school portals and student progress apps.

text: What is your response to a potential data leak alert?

options.text: Locking down cloud network routes, shutting ports, and inspecting VPC traffic logs.
Revoking API token access, rotating secret keys, and checking endpoint logs.
Checking if models are leaks training features in prediction outputs.
Drafting emergency incident statements and communicating with legal advisors.

text: How do you manage software updates for security?

options.text: Verifying that design component updates match visual library layouts.
Scheduling software update timelines to avoid business sale downtime.
Testing updates in medical test sandboxes to protect clinical software.
Automating container updates and running vulnerability scanner tools.

text: What is your strategy for securing api keys?

options.text: Securing private model endpoint credentials to prevent prediction spam.

Restricting database API credentials to read-only roles when querying tables.
Using cloud vault key managers and rotating passwords automatically.

Scaling database connection pools and using Redis caching for API data.
